17: 	// NoProto is used when the connection isn't actually TLS
18: 	NoProto = "(none)"
19: 
20: 	// DefaultProto is used when there is an ALPN we don't actually know about.
21: 	// If no protos are specified on an incoming TLS connection we will first
22: 	// look for a proto of ""; if not found, will use DefaultProto. On a
23: 	// connection that has protos defined, we will look for that proto first,
24: 	// then DefaultProto.
25: 	DefaultProto = "(*)"
26: )

28: type bufferedConn struct {

31: }

37: type muxedListener struct {
38: 	connMutex *sync.RWMutex
39: 	ctx       context.Context
40: 	addr      net.Addr
41: 	proto     string
42: 	tlsConf   *tls.Config
43: 	connCh    chan net.Conn
44: 	closed    bool
45: 	closeFunc func()
46: 	closeOnce *sync.Once
47: }
48: 
49: type ALPNMux struct {
50: 	ctx    context.Context
51: 	baseLn net.Listener
52: 	cancel context.CancelFunc
53: 	muxMap *sync.Map
54: }
55: 
56: func New(baseLn net.Listener) *ALPNMux {
57: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
58: 	ret := &ALPNMux{
59: 		ctx:    ctx,
60: 		cancel: cancel,
61: 		muxMap: new(sync.Map),
62: 		baseLn: baseLn,
63: 	}
64: 	go ret.accept()
65: 	return ret
66: }
67: 
68: func (l *ALPNMux) Addr() net.Addr {
69: 	return l.baseLn.Addr()
70: }
71: 
72: func (l *ALPNMux) Close() error {
73: 	return l.baseLn.Close()
74: }
75: 
76: func (l *ALPNMux) RegisterProto(proto string, tlsConf *tls.Config) (net.Listener, error) {
77: 	const op = "alpnmux.(ALPNMux).RegisterProto"
78: 	switch proto {
79: 	case NoProto:
80: 		if tlsConf != nil {
81: 			return nil, errors.New("tls config cannot be non-nil when using NoProto")
82: 		}
83: 	default:
84: 		if tlsConf == nil {
85: 			return nil, errors.New("nil tls config given")
86: 		}
87: 	}
88: 	sub := &muxedListener{
89: 		connMutex: new(sync.RWMutex),
90: 		ctx:       l.ctx,
91: 		addr:      l.baseLn.Addr(),
92: 		proto:     proto,
93: 		tlsConf:   tlsConf,
94: 		connCh:    make(chan net.Conn),
95: 		closeOnce: new(sync.Once),
96: 	}
97: 	_, loaded := l.muxMap.LoadOrStore(proto, sub)
98: 	if loaded {
99: 		close(sub.connCh)
100: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("proto %q already registered", proto)
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	sub.closeFunc = func() {
104: 		go l.UnregisterProto(proto)
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	return sub, nil
108: }
109: 
110: func (l *ALPNMux) UnregisterProto(proto string) {
111: 	const op = "alpnmux.(ALPNMux).UnregisterProto"
112: 	val, ok := l.muxMap.Load(proto)
113: 	if !ok {
114: 		return
115: 	}
116: 	ml := val.(*muxedListener)
117: 	ml.closeOnce.Do(func() {
118: 		ml.connMutex.Lock()
119: 		defer ml.connMutex.Unlock()
120: 		ml.closed = true
121: 		close(ml.connCh)
122: 	})
123: 	l.muxMap.Delete(proto)
124: }
125: 
126: func (l *ALPNMux) GetListener(proto string) net.Listener {
127: 	val, ok := l.muxMap.Load(proto)
128: 	if !ok || val == nil {
129: 		val, ok = l.muxMap.Load(DefaultProto)
130: 		if !ok || val == nil {
131: 			return nil
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 	return val.(*muxedListener)
135: }
136: 
137: func (l *ALPNMux) getConfigForClient(hello *tls.ClientHelloInfo) (*tls.Config, error) {
138: 	const op = "alpnmux.(ALPNMux).getConfigForClient"
139: 	var ret *tls.Config
140: 
141: 	supportedProtos := hello.SupportedProtos
142: 	if len(hello.SupportedProtos) == 0 {
143: 		supportedProtos = append(supportedProtos, "")
144: 	}
145: 	for _, proto := range supportedProtos {
146: 		val, ok := l.muxMap.Load(proto)
147: 		if !ok {
148: 			continue
149: 		}
150: 		ret = val.(*muxedListener).tlsConf
151: 	}
152: 	if ret == nil {
153: 		val, ok := l.muxMap.Load(DefaultProto)
154: 		if ok && val != nil {
155: 			ret = val.(*muxedListener).tlsConf
156: 		}
157: 	}
158: 	if ret == nil {
159: 		return nil, errors.New("no tls configuration available for any client protos")
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	// If the TLS config we found has its own lookup function, chain to it
163: 	if ret.GetConfigForClient != nil {
164: 		return ret.GetConfigForClient(hello)
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	return ret, nil
168: }
169: 
170: func (l *ALPNMux) accept() {
171: 	const op = "alpnmux.(ALPNMux).accept"
172: 	ctx := context.TODO()
173: 	baseTLSConf := &tls.Config{
174: 		GetConfigForClient: l.getConfigForClient,
175: 	}
176: 	for {
177: 		conn, err := l.baseLn.Accept()
178: 		if err != nil {
179: 			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "use of closed network connection") {
180: 				l.cancel()
181: 				return
182: 			}
183: 		}
184: 		if conn == nil {
185: 			continue
186: 		}
187: 
188: 		// Do the rest in a goroutine so that a timeout in e.g. handshaking
189: 		// doesn't block acceptance of the next connection
190: 		go func() {
191: 			bufConn := &bufferedConn{
192: 				Conn:   conn,
193: 				buffer: bufio.NewReader(conn),
194: 			}
195: 			peeked, err := bufConn.buffer.Peek(3)
196: 			if err != nil {
197: 				bufConn.Close()
198: 				return
199: 			}
200: 			switch {
201: 			// First byte should always be a handshake, second byte a 3, and
202: 			// third can be 3 or 1 depending on the implementation
203: 			case peeked[0] != 0x16 || peeked[1] != 0x03 || (peeked[2] != 0x03 && peeked[2] != 0x01):
204: 				val, ok := l.muxMap.Load(NoProto)
205: 				if !ok {
206: 					bufConn.Close()
207: 					return
208: 				}
209: 				ml := val.(*muxedListener)
210: 				ml.connMutex.RLock()
211: 				if !ml.closed {
212: 					ml.connCh <- bufConn
213: 				}
214: 				ml.connMutex.RUnlock()
215: 
216: 			default:
217: 				tlsConn := tls.Server(bufConn, baseTLSConf)
218: 				if err := tlsConn.Handshake(); err != nil {
219: 					closeErr := tlsConn.Close()
220: 					if closeErr != nil {
221: 						event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error handshaking connection", "addr", conn.RemoteAddr(), "close_error", closeErr))
222: 					}
223: 					return
224: 				}
225: 				negProto := tlsConn.ConnectionState().NegotiatedProtocol
226: 				val, ok := l.muxMap.Load(negProto)
227: 				if !ok {
228: 					val, ok = l.muxMap.Load(DefaultProto)
229: 					if !ok {
230: 						tlsConn.Close()
231: 						return
232: 					}
233: 				}
234: 				ml := val.(*muxedListener)
235: 				ml.connMutex.RLock()
236: 				if !ml.closed {
237: 					ml.connCh <- tlsConn
238: 				}
239: 				ml.connMutex.RUnlock()
240: 			}
241: 		}()
242: 	}
243: }
244: 
245: func (m *muxedListener) Accept() (net.Conn, error) {
246: 	for {
247: 		select {
248: 		case <-m.ctx.Done():
249: 			// Wouldn't it be so much better if this error was an exported
250: 			// const from Go...
251: 			m.closeFunc()
252: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("accept proto %s: use of closed network connection", m.proto)
253: 		case conn, ok := <-m.connCh:
254: 			if !ok {
255: 				// Channel closed
256: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("accept proto %s: use of closed network connection", m.proto)
257: 			}
258: 			if conn == nil {
259: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("accept proto %s: nil connection received", m.proto)
260: 			}
261: 			return conn, nil
262: 		}
263: 	}
264: }
265: 
266: func (m *muxedListener) Close() error {
267: 	m.closeFunc()
268: 	return nil
269: }
270: 
271: func (m *muxedListener) Addr() net.Addr {
272: 	return m.addr
273: }
